statement: Next-generation accelerators — NVIDIA Vera Rubin, AMD Helios (MI400-series), AWS Trainium 3 and Google TPU — are all moving into mass production simultaneously, expanding datacenter accelerator supply into 2H26.
why: Simultaneous mass-production ramps across GPUs and custom ASICs expand alternative accelerator supply and intensify competition beyond NVIDIA merchant GPUs.

impact.mechanism: Concurrent mass production of GPUs and custom ASICs increases available accelerator supply and competitive alternatives into the second half of 2026.

evidence.excerpt: stronger 2H26 server shipments as driven by simultaneous mass-production ramps across Nvidia's next-gen Vera Rubin GPU platform, AMD's Helios rack (MI400-series), AWS Trainium 3, and Google TPU custom silicon

confidence.basis: Trade press (secondary), free excerpt; capacity detail paywalled.
